name: Boardwalk Gate
short_desc: Rotten planks span a gap over dark water. A rusted gate hangs open.
long_desc: The boardwalk groans under your weight. Ahead, a gate of bog-iron leans on one hinge; beyond it, the swamp proper.

name: Witch's Hut
short_desc: A hut on stork-legs leans out over the water, smoke curling from a crooked chimney.
long_desc: Bone wind-chimes clack in no breeze you can feel. The door is painted with three overlapping circles in what might be berry juice. Something inside is humming a lullaby, off-key, and waiting.

name: Old Causeway
short_desc: A sunken stone road rises out of the muck, the far end vanishing into fog.
long_desc: The causeway's flagstones are green with algae, and every third one is missing. You can see wagon-ruts older than the kingdom, if it still is a kingdom, worn into the survivors.

name: Sunken Grove
short_desc: A ring of rotten willows, knee-deep in black water.
long_desc: Moss drips from every branch. The water ripples once, then settles. Something down there is watching the reflection of the sky.

name: Leech Ford
short_desc: Brown water laps across a half-submerged log bridge.
long_desc: The log is slick and the water beyond it moves wrong — sluggishly, in the opposite direction to the current. You can see dark shapes drifting just beneath the surface.

name: Trailhead
short_desc: A beaten footpath enters the trees under a weathered wooden arch.
long_desc: Someone has carved a crude sun into the arch and blackened it with pitch. The path beyond is soft with pine needles, and a jay calls twice somewhere deeper in.

name: Mossy Clearing
short_desc: A bowl of sun-dappled moss with a flat stone at its center.
long_desc: Yellow light falls in slow columns through the canopy. The stone is warm. There is a single acorn balanced upright on it, which is the sort of thing you notice and do not forget.

name: Deerfall Stream
short_desc: A shallow stream chatters over white stones between the trees.
long_desc: A doe once fell here, by the look of the clean-picked ribs on the bank. The water is cold enough to ache your teeth and clear enough to count the pebbles.

name: Hunter's Blind
short_desc: A platform of lashed branches squats in a broad oak, rope ladder dangling.
long_desc: The ladder has been recently used — the bark is scraped fresh. On the platform lies a half-eaten apple, a scrap of waxed paper, and a single arrow fletched with a feather you don't recognize. Whoever was up here left in a hurry.

name: Thornwall
short_desc: A wall of black thorns stretches in both directions, taller than a man.
long_desc: The thorns are older than the trees around them — polished, almost metallic, woven by something patient. A narrow gap near the ground has been chewed wider by some low animal. You could crawl through if you were willing to bleed.

name: Market Square
short_desc: An empty market square, stall-frames picked clean, cobbles dark with old rain.
long_desc: Handbills flap against a shuttered guardpost — something about a curfew, a price, a name crossed out. A child's wooden top lies abandoned at the foot of a dry fountain, still spinning as if just dropped.

name: Narrow Alley
short_desc: An alley so tight your shoulders brush both walls, running between leaning tenements.
long_desc: The walls are patched with a century of posters, most of them faded to a single brown suggestion. A cat watches you from a sill above, then isn't there. Something at the far end moves, or was moved.

name: Drowned Cathedral
short_desc: A cathedral's nave stands knee-deep in silent water, pews floating in rows.
long_desc: The altar is gone; only its stepped pedestal remains, and atop it a single wet candle burns without a flame. The stained glass is mostly out, and what remains throws blue and green ripples across the vaulted stones. You can hear a choir, very faintly, from the wrong direction.

name: The Crooked Inn
short_desc: A common room leaning two degrees off plumb, hearth banked low.
long_desc: The beams are so warped the chandelier hangs visibly askew, and yet the mugs on the long table sit level as if the world is the thing that's wrong. A pot of something brown and onion-smelling mutters on the hob. A door swings in no draft.

name: Gallows Square
short_desc: A gibbet stands in a cracked plaza, its cage empty, its chain cut clean.
long_desc: Someone has scratched three names into the wood of the post, and then a fourth name over the top, harder, as if to claim it. The flagstones nearest the gibbet are scrubbed cleaner than the rest. Pigeons will not land here.

name: Cave Mouth
short_desc: A low limestone arch opens into darkness that smells of wet stone.
long_desc: Bats have lived here: the floor crunches. A cold draft pushes steadily out of the hole, carrying the sound of water somewhere far below. The daylight behind you seems dimmer than it ought to be already.

name: Dripstone Hall
short_desc: A forest of stone columns grown from ceiling to floor.
long_desc: Each drip takes a decade and each column took an age. The sound is everywhere — tk, tk, tk — and your own footsteps come back from six directions at once. In the dimness, every column looks briefly like a figure.

name: Blind Pool
short_desc: A still pool fills the chamber wall-to-wall, glass-flat and black.
long_desc: The water is so clear that when you look down you can see nothing — there is no bottom in any direction your light reaches. Pale things without eyes drift in slow arcs, and one of them is looking back anyway.

name: Glowworm Cathedral
short_desc: A vast vault lit by a ceiling of living constellations.
long_desc: Ten thousand glowworms map out a sky that has never seen the real one, and beneath their soft green light an altar has been carved straight out of the living rock. Something was left on it long ago. Something else took most of it away.

name: Collapsed Gallery
short_desc: A tunnel half-choked with fallen stone, a narrow way forced through.
long_desc: The collapse is recent enough that the dust still hangs in your lantern beam. Someone smaller than you has picked through it and left a trail of scuffs you could follow if you had to. One of the fallen blocks has a rune on it, worn almost smooth.

name: Broken Archway
short_desc: Two-thirds of a triumphal arch still stands, grass on top.
long_desc: The keystone is gone and the arch should have come down centuries ago, but it hasn't. A relief along the inner face shows a procession — the figures' heads have all been carefully chipped away, one by one.

name: Sunken Library
short_desc: Shelves of books stand ankle-deep in still water, spines intact, pages gone.
long_desc: Every book has been drowned and then kept — the spines are fine leather with fine letters, but the pages have been dissolved and pressed back in as a single grey brick of pulp. One book on a lectern is dry, the only dry thing in the room, and open to a page you cannot quite focus on.

name: Overgrown Plaza
short_desc: A plaza of cracked tile, a young oak forcing its way through the fountain.
long_desc: The fountain's figure has fallen and lies in pieces, each piece wrapped in roots as if the tree were eating it on purpose. Wind moves through the old colonnade and it sounds like breathing.

name: Toppled Obelisk
short_desc: A black obelisk lies across the road, inscriptions facing down.
long_desc: Whatever the obelisk said, someone very much wanted you not to read it. It was thrown, not fallen — the bedding-stone it came from is thirty yards uphill and the fall-line is straight. The underside, where you can't see, is warm to the touch.

name: Drained Cistern
short_desc: A vast empty cistern, echo-deep, high-water marks ringing the walls.
long_desc: The stair descends in a spiral along the wall, and at the bottom a single puddle remains, perfectly circular, refilling visibly from no source you can find. The air smells of minerals and old iron.

name: Windcut Dunes
short_desc: Long dunes stretch to the horizon, the wind carving the crests into blades.
long_desc: The sand is fine as flour and the wind never stops. Every few steps you crest a rise and see the same empty distance, except one direction, where a dark smudge might be a tower or a trick of the heat.

name: Bone Garden
short_desc: Great ribs of something enormous stand up out of the sand like a grove.
long_desc: The ribs are taller than the tallest tree and polished by a century of grit. Someone, more recently, has hung small bundles of cloth from each one. They twist in the wind. They do not unravel.

name: Glass Oasis
short_desc: A ring of palms turned to green glass, a glass pool at their feet.
long_desc: Something struck here and the heat of it cooked the oasis whole. The palms ring like bells when the wind catches them. The pool is still water, somehow, cupped inside a bowl of fused sand, and the sand at the rim has your reflection in it before you've arrived.

name: Buried Caravan
short_desc: Wagon-tops jut from the sand, canvas shredded, bones in the traces.
long_desc: Whoever dug through here dug fast and took what was small and left what was heavy. A chest sits open, padded, shaped for something the size of a child. The sand around it has not drifted back in, which is strange.

name: Sunstone Gate
short_desc: A free-standing gate of red stone, no wall, casting a shadow it shouldn't.
long_desc: The gate stands alone on the flat, and its shadow falls across the sand toward the midday sun — the wrong way, always. Passing under it is said to be a choice and not a motion. The stone is warm on one side and cold on the other.

name: Scree Slope
short_desc: A long slope of loose stone, every step a small avalanche.
long_desc: The scree is the color of old bone and every piece of it wants to be further down than it is. The path — if path is the word — is picked out by a line of cairns, half of which have fallen.

name: Wind Notch
short_desc: A knife-thin pass where the wind screams through a gap in the rock.
long_desc: The wind here is a continuous, pitched note, and you feel it in your teeth before your ears. Ice has grown along the north face in the shape of reaching fingers. On the south face, there is nothing, as if the wind has scoured even the dark away.

name: Frozen Shrine
short_desc: A small shrine in the lee of a cliff, every surface furred with hoarfrost.
long_desc: A dozen stone bowls stand in a row, each one full of clear ice. At the back of the shrine a figure sits cross-legged, patient under a blanket of frost, and you cannot tell how long it has been patient.

name: Dragon's Nest
short_desc: A wide stone bowl atop the peak, strewn with scorched bone and bright coin.
long_desc: The nest is older than any story about dragons and the dragon is older than the nest. Heat shimmers off the black rock even with no sun on it. A single tooth the length of your forearm sits on top of the hoard; it's been placed there, not dropped.

name: Rope Bridge
short_desc: A rope bridge sags across a chasm, half its planks missing.
long_desc: The drop is the kind you don't quite look into. The bridge sways of its own accord, and on the far side a single mitten, child-sized, is caught on a rail. The wind, far below, is doing a good impression of voices.

name: Threshold Stair
short_desc: A wide stair descends under a lintel carved with closed eyes.
long_desc: Each step is worn deeper in the center by ten thousand bearers and no climbers. The carved eyes on the lintel are precisely shut, not peaceful — shut the way you shut a door against something.

name: Ossuary Aisle
short_desc: Walls stacked with skulls, each niche carefully filled.
long_desc: The skulls are sorted by size and age, and a few still wear rings of copper wire on their teeth. Someone comes here and tends them — a few are freshly dusted, and there are flower stems in three of the eye-sockets, not long dried.

name: Sealed Vault
short_desc: A round chamber whose door has been sealed from the outside, and welded from the inside.
long_desc: The vault has been closed twice, by two different people, in two different centuries, for two different reasons. In the center stands a sarcophagus of black glass, and inside the glass, the dark is moving. There is no lid, because the dark is the lid.

name: Silent Gallery
short_desc: A long hall of carved effigies, every mouth stopped with a bronze coin.
long_desc: Each effigy lies on its own slab, hands folded, a bronze coin laid edge-on in the carved lips. Your footsteps do not echo here; the stone eats sound. One coin is missing. One of the effigies is breathing.

name: Reliquary Crypt
short_desc: Empty shrines along both walls, the relics long gone, the plinths still lit.
long_desc: Candles burn in alcoves that have held nothing for a hundred years — unless what they held was the burning. Every reliquary has been levered open and emptied; a few still have the indentation where the bone or the splinter or the lock of hair rested.
